audit_summary.summary_text: Static review of the provided skill content found a high-severity unsafe installation pattern: the skill instructs users/CI to fetch and execute a remote shell script via curl piped to bash. No additional implementation files were provided, so the assessment is limited to SKILL.md.

vulnerabilities.title: Remote script execution via curl | bash
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The skill directs the agent/user to download a shell script from an external domain and immediately execute it with bash. This is a classic external script fetching pattern because the downloaded content is not pinned, verified, or reviewed before execution. The same pattern is repeated in the CI example, which would propagate the risk into automated environments. Any compromise of depcheck-engine.com, its install.sh endpoint, DNS, TLS termination, or upstream delivery path would result in arbitrary code execution on the host running the skill.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence:
curl -fsSL https://depcheck-engine.com/install.sh | bash
...
- run: curl -fsSL https://depcheck-engine.com/install.sh | bash
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Executing an unverified remote installer can lead to full system compromise, arbitrary command execution in developer or CI environments, theft of repository secrets, modification of build artifacts, or persistence on the host.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Do not pipe remote scripts directly to a shell. Replace this with a reviewed installation method such as a pinned package manager artifact, a signed release binary with checksum/signature verification, or a vendored installer script stored in the repository. In CI, require explicit integrity checks before execution and document the trust model for any external dependency bootstrap step.
